2603.14867.title: Sample-Efficient Hypergradient Estimation for Decentralized Bi-Level Reinforcement Learning
2603.14867.paper_summary: Many strategic decision-making problems, such as environment design for warehouse robots, can be naturally formulated as bi-level reinforcement learning (RL), where a leader agent optimizes its objective while a follower solves a Markov decision process (MDP) conditioned on the leader's decisions. In many situations, a fundamental challenge arises when the leader cannot intervene in the follower's optimization process; it can only observe the optimization outcome. We address this decentralized setting by deriving the hypergradient of the leader's objective, i.e., the gradient of the leader's strategy that accounts for changes in the follower's optimal policy. Unlike prior hypergradient-based methods that require extensive data for repeated state visits or rely on gradient estimators whose complexity can increase substantially with the high-dimensional leader's decision space, we leverage the Boltzmann covariance trick to derive an alternative hypergradient formulation. This enables efficient hypergradient estimation solely from interaction samples, even when the leader's decision space is high-dimensional. Additionally, to our knowledge, this is the first method that enables hypergradient-based optimization for 2-player Markov games in decentralized settings. Experiments highlight the impact of hypergradient updates and demonstrate our method's effectiveness in both discrete and continuous state tasks.
2603.14867.paper_summary_zh: 許多戰略決策問題，例如倉庫機器人的環境設計，可以自然地表述為雙層增強學習（RL），其中領導代理優化其目標，而跟隨者根據領導者的決策解決馬爾可夫決策過程（MDP）。在許多情況下，當領導者無法介入跟隨者的優化過程時，會出現一個基本挑戰；它只能觀察優化結果。我們通過推導領導者目標的超梯度來解決這種去中心化的設置，即考慮到跟隨者最優策略變化的領導者策略的梯度。與以往需要大量數據以進行重複狀態訪問的基於超梯度的方法或依賴於隨著高維領導者決策空間而顯著增加複雜性的梯度估計器的方法不同，我們利用玻爾茲曼協方差技巧推導出一種替代的超梯度表述。這使得即使在領導者的決策空間是高維的情況下，也能僅通過互動樣本有效地估計超梯度。此外，據我們所知，這是第一種能夠在去中心化設置中實現基於超梯度的優化的2人馬爾可夫遊戲的方法。實驗突顯了超梯度更新的影響，並展示了我們的方法在離散和連續狀態任務中的有效性。
2603.14867.author: Mikoto Kudo et.al.
2603.14867.authors: Mikoto Kudo, Takumi Tanabe, Akifumi Wachi, Youhei Akimoto
